calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Continued To Nov-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Intention To Move And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order And Enter Another And Different Order
Off Calendar Jan-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tom Lyle, Accountant for the Trust in Propria Persona. John Leutza, Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor Beneficiaries. Christina Gallagher for Flora Lucas (Trustee).

Ms. Gallagher stated that they were applying the wrong standard to the fees, that there were no objections to the fee request and that the only standard was the trust instrument. Furthermore, Ms. Gallagher stated that the trust instrument did not set guidelines, that fees were allowed, that there was no evidence of the fees being unreasonable, that local rule 14.95 applied as the trust was never before the Court and that there was nothing to refute it. Mr. Leutza stated that the fees were not unreasonable. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

Notice Of Intention To Move And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Order And Enter Another And Different Order
Off Calendar Nov-20-2008 Continued To Jan-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor Beneficiaries. Christina Gallagher for and with Flora Lucas (Trustee). Tom Lyell (Accountant for the Trustee).

Ms. Gallagher stated that there was an error on the last order, that the incorrect formula and wrong numbers were used for value of the estate and provided time logs to support the request for fees. Mr. Leutza did not object to the Court's review of the formula. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On The Accounting
Off Calendar Aug-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza, Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor Beneficiaries. Christina Gallagher for and with Flora Lucas (Trustee). Ariella Fioranelli (Neice).

The Court stated that it had received communication from the Beneficiaries. Ms. Gallagher stated that communication was inappropriate, that there was no opposition and that Ms. Fioranelli had no standing as she was not a beneficiary. The Court allowed Ms. Fioranelli to speak as the Husband (Beneficiary) stated that she was speaking on his behalf. Ms. Fioranelli raised the issue regarding the $60,000.00 distribution, stated that Mr. Leutza had no authority to pay those funds to Ms. Lucas and requested time to revisit the flower trust stating that 90 years was a long time. Ms. Gallagher stated that the accountant did the returns, that she faxed a declaration in response to the Examiner's notes, that the flower trust funds were passed over to the Trustee and that it was not her affair. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Nelson for Flora Lucas. John Lutza (Guardian Ad Litem). Ariela Fioranelli (Wife of Beneficiary).

Ms. Fioranelli stated that the request for additional compensation was incurred due to Ms. Lucas switching attorneys, that the tax penalties should be the responsiblilty of Ms. Lucas and that the flower fund should have been set up immediately. In addition, she stated that some of the things Ms. Lucas charged the estate for were inappropriate and that she only received $25,000.00 with no interest. The Court stated that this issue was not before the Court and that it would come up in the accounting. Ms. Nelson stated that they were looking to the Court to determine the distribution plan and the issue regarding paying of the taxes. In addition, she stated that she believed that about 30 of the beneficiaries replied to the equal shares. The petition for instructions was taken under submission. The issue regarding the accounting was continued to July 29, 2008 for receipts and for adjusting of the accounting.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Appointment Of Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Apr-22-2008 Granted Per Written Order
